name: baml
description: Teaches Claude Code to write idiomatic BAML (the baml_language rewrite). Four example-dense skills: core (the LANGUAGE — basically TypeScript with snake_case methods and `name type` fields — plus the LLM DSL that desugars into it), bridges (calling BAML from a host language: subprocess bridge first, generated SDK with baml_core pinning second), serving (HTTP servers and long-lived processes — host owns the socket, BAML owns the typed handler), and testing (testset/test, assert.*, token-free LLM testing via Fn$parse). Every example is verified against the shipped CLI; defers to `baml describe` for everything else.
